name: Iceland (1944–1991)

colors.symbolism: the Atlantic Ocean and the mountains

description: The 1944–1991 civil flag of Iceland — a deep cobalt-blue field bearing a white-fimbriated red Nordic cross. Identical in pattern to the current flag but with the original (un-standardized) shade of blue.
history: When Iceland became a republic on 17 June 1944, the kingdom-era flag (adopted 1915, made the state flag in 1918) was retained. The exact colours were not legally standardized until 1991, when the blue was officially set to a darker shade.
